result.root_cause: Compound failure caused by simultaneous deployment of two configuration changes: (1) connection pool reduced from 100 to 50 connections, pushing utilization to 96% and causing connection starvation (database_connection_errors z-score 3.2, query_timeout_count z-score 2.9), and (2) transaction isolation level changed to READ_UNCOMMITTED. The pool reduction created resource exhaustion and retry storms (retry_attempt_count z-score 1.3), while the isolation level change allowed dirty reads during these retries, resulting in 48,302 corrupted records (data_validation_failures z-score 4.8). This exact scenario was documented in past incident INC-1923 with 0.94 similarity, which showed that small pool + aggressive timeouts + READ_UNCOMMITTED isolation causes connection errors, query timeouts, and cascading data corruption failures. The pool constraint is the primary trigger, with isolation level acting as an amplifier.

fix_options.title: Full Deployment Rollback with Data Repair
commands: kubectl set env deployment/order-service DB_POOL_SIZE=100 DB_ISOLATION_LEVEL=READ_COMMITTED
fix_options.rollback: If rollback causes issues: kubectl rollout undo deployment/order-service (reverts to problematic version). Then apply fix option 2 instead.

fix_options.title: Targeted Configuration Fix Without Full Rollback
fix_options.rollback: Revert configuration: kubectl set env deployment/order-service DB_POOL_SIZE=50 DB_ISOLATION_LEVEL=READ_UNCOMMITTED && kubectl rollout restart deployment/order-service

fix_options.title: Gradual Pool Increase with Monitoring
commands: sleep 60 && kubectl set env deployment/order-service DB_POOL_SIZE=75
kubectl exec -it deployment/order-service -- psql -c 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE validation_status=\'FAILED\' AND updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL \'1 hour\''
fix_options.rollback: Reduce pool incrementally: kubectl set env deployment/order-service DB_POOL_SIZE=75, wait 2 min, then DB_POOL_SIZE=50. Revert isolation: kubectl set env deployment/order-service DB_ISOLATION_LEVEL=READ_UNCOMMITTED
